RULING:
A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling -- Defendant NICOLE FAUCHER's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT -- granted. No triable issue re: disclosure, principal. Defendant is to prepare an order pursuant to CCP 437c(g). (DAG/301/DR) Reporter: Joe Vickstein, CSR 4780

Osc Sanctions Fta At Msc. (To Be Heard At 8:45 Am In Dept. 504 On Sept-23-2002 Before Hon. Robert L. Dondero

Osc Nicole Faucher
Off Calendar Oct-08-2002 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: The cause is advanced from October 15, 2002 OSC hearing of Nicole Faucher, at request of Ms. Faucher. Nicole Faucher appeared represented by her counsel, Greg Fox, Esq. Following an explanation by Ms. Faucher of her reasons for failing to appear at the Mandatory Settlement Conference on September 5, 2002 in Dept. 504, the Court imposed sanctions in the amount of $300.00 to be paid to the Clerk's Office within 24 hours.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-17-2002 Failure To Post Jury Fees

Osc Sanctions Fta At Msc. (To Be Heard At 8:45 Am In Dept. 504 On Sept-23-2002 Before Hon. Robert L. Dondero
Off Calendar Sep-23-2002 Continued To Oct-15-02 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 504
MINI MINUTES: The cause came on regular calendar this day for OSC re sanctions. Michael Wenzel, Esq. appeared for Ms Faucher, who did not appear. The Court, having been informed that the trial in the cause is continued to October 15, 2002, therefore continued the OSC to October 15, 2002 at 8:45 AM for Ms. Faucher to appear and show cause, if any there be, why she should not be sanctioned for failing to appear at the prior mandatory settlement conference.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-20-2002 Continued To Oct-15-02 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONTINUE COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Sep-20-2002 Granted Per Written Order

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-05-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: The cause came on regular calendar this day for settlement conference. Counsel for Plaintiff, Sydney Fairbairn, Esq. appeared. There being no appearance by Pro Per Defendant, Capspring; Ms. N. Faucher, court issues OSC why defendant should not be sanctioned and continues the matter for hearing on 9-23-02 in Dept. 504 at 8:45 AM.
